text:
There wasn impossible things. That was what Uncle Toby used to talk about. One impossible thing he loved was old Marquez's gardens. They looked just like something out of a painting. Every plant seemed to know just what to do to look like something else; a rooster was really a beautiful peacock. Or was it just my imagination? An owl was surely a glossy night hawk. Or perhaps—

No. It was Marquez.

That was why Uncle Toby loved him. So did I. Maybe that’s why I’m here.

They’d go to these things every summer.

And the cats? They were just as interested.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
